text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members frequently integrate their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and a set of automated tests. This is done through a CI pipeline, which runs every time new code is submitted. The main benefit is catching issues early—problems are detected minutes after they are introduced, rather than weeks later. By running tests automatically, developers can fix bugs quickly, reducing the time and effort needed for debugging. CI helps teams maintain a stable codebase and work more efficiently, especially in larger projects.
